schema.title: Response Sign In Api V1 Auth Signin Post

post.description:
Email an ``email_2fa_login`` code for the user behind a valid login challenge.

The challenge token is validated first (it proves the password already passed),
so this can't be used for user enumeration; rate-limited per source IP. To avoid
enumeration, a user without email-2FA still yields a generic ``sent: True`` (no
code is actually sent).

Second step of login-2FA: exchange the challenge + a second-factor code for the
token pair. ``method=totp`` (default) reuses TotpService.verify (brute-force
lockout + recovery-code fallback); ``method=email`` consumes an emailed
``email_2fa_login`` code requested via /2fa/login/email/request.

Email an ``email_2fa_step_up`` code for a user with email-2FA enrolled. The
code is then submitted to /2fa/step-up with ``method=email``. Rate-limited.

Exchange a fresh second-factor code for a short-lived step-up token used to
authorize critical actions. Accepts either a TOTP/recovery code (``method=totp``,
default) or an emailed code (``method=email``). The minted token is
factor-agnostic, so ``require_step_up`` and the gated-endpoint list are unchanged.

Begin email-2FA enrollment: email a code the user must confirm (verify-on-
enroll — the account email is not implicitly trusted). Requires Resend to be
configured (503 otherwise). Throttled per user to prevent email-bombing.

Confirm enrollment with the emailed code → activates email-2FA. Wrong/expired
code → 400; per-factor lockout → 429.

T20 (W11c): email a one-time confirmation code for a critical action.

Disabled (503) when Resend isn't configured — existing flows are unaffected.
Throttled per user to prevent email-bombing.

post.summary: Promote a sandbox strategy to live (step-up required)
Demote a live strategy back to sandbox (step-up required)
Manually flatten every open auto-trade position
post.description: Two-step destructive operation. First call with ``confirm: false`` returns HTTP 412 with a preview of every position that would be closed (symbol, side, quantity, conditional-order count). Re-send with ``confirm: true`` to actually cancel TP/SL and market-close. Independent from ``/auto-trade/stop``: this does not flip ``is_running``.

412.description: Confirmation required. Body contains an ``AutoTradeClosePreview`` payload.

404.description: Auto-trade config not found for the given scope.

get.summary: Strategy health score (composite of win rate, drawdown, PnL, stability)
Post-trade execution trace (signal → close timeline) for a position
Get auto-trade positions with PnL summary
Aggregated portfolio view across all strategies
get.description: Returns one row per :class:`AutoTradeConfig` owned by the user, with realized/unrealized PnL, open position count, and live sub-account balance. Balance fetches run in parallel; one failing exchange surfaces as ``balance_error`` on that row instead of failing the whole response.

post.summary: Start every enabled strategy for the user
post.description: Bulk variant of ``/auto-trade/play``. Skips configs that are disabled or already running. One failure does not abort the others — every config gets its own per-row outcome.

post.summary: Stop every running strategy for the user
post.description: Bulk variant of ``/auto-trade/stop``. Only flips ``is_running=false`` — open positions are deliberately not closed. Use ``/auto-trade/close-positions`` (per account) when you want to flatten.

get.summary: USDT balance for one strategy's sub-account
get.description: Powers the per-strategy budget card. Returns the free/total USDT balance fetched live from the exchange. ``error`` non-null means the fetch failed; the dashboard treats it as 'balance unavailable'.

post.summary: Generate a one-time Telegram deep link

get.description:
Push the authenticated user's streamable events (kill-switch, KPI-guard,
portfolio-DD halt, data-stale, …) as Server-Sent Events.

Note: browser ``EventSource`` cannot set an Authorization header, so the
frontend passes the bearer token via the proxy/cookie layer. The generator
stops cleanly on client disconnect and on server shutdown (CancelledError).

AccountBalanceResponse.title: AccountBalanceResponse
AccountBalanceResponse.description:
USDT balance snapshot for one strategy's sub-account.

Drives the per-strategy budget card. ``error`` non-null means the adapter
call failed; the dashboard treats those as 'balance unavailable' and
falls back to local margin-used figures.

AgentFreshnessRead.title: AgentFreshnessRead
AgentFreshnessRead.description: Current data-freshness snapshot for one (profile, agent) (W8 — T3.3).

entry_side_lock_enabled.title: Entry Side Lock Enabled
entry_side_lock_enabled.description: Block entries whose side contradicts ai_trend direction (phase 1).

atr_scaling_enabled.title: Atr Scaling Enabled
atr_scaling_enabled.description: Scale the SL ATR multiplier by ai_trend.strength (phase 2).

rsi_scaling_enabled.title: Rsi Scaling Enabled
rsi_scaling_enabled.description: Shift RSI watcher thresholds by ai_trend direction (phase 3).

stale_max_minutes.title: Stale Max Minutes
stale_max_minutes.description: Maximum age of the ai_trend record (minutes). Older records trigger fail-open fallback.

min_strength.title: Min Strength
min_strength.description: Minimum ai_trend.strength below which the overlay treats the signal as no-op.

atr_scale_range.title: Atr Scale Range
atr_scale_range.description: (min, max) multiplier applied to the base ATR multiplier. Bounds the runtime override.

rsi_max_shift.title: Rsi Max Shift
rsi_max_shift.description: Maximum absolute shift (in points) applied to RSI thresholds by the overlay.

AiOverlayConfig.title: AiOverlayConfig
AiOverlayConfig.description:
Per-user overlay configuration stored as JSON on AutoTradeConfig.

All flags default to ``False`` so existing users see no behaviour
change until they explicitly opt in. ``stale_max_minutes`` matches the
plan-mandated 4-hour freshness ceiling for ai_trend.

AiOverlayConfigUpdateRequest.title: AiOverlayConfigUpdateRequest
AiOverlayConfigUpdateRequest.description: API request — partial update; missing fields keep their current value.

start_time.title: Start Time
start_time.description: Optional inclusive OHLCV start time (ISO datetime).

end_time.title: End Time
end_time.description: Optional inclusive OHLCV end time (ISO datetime).

candles.title: Candles
candles.description: Optional OHLCV override from client. If omitted, backend fetches candles by symbol/timeframe/bars.

account_id.title: Account Id
account_id.description: Optional account scope. Required only if the user owns more than one auto-trade config. Otherwise the unique config is resolved from ``user_id`` alone.

confirm.title: Confirm
confirm.description: Must be true to actually close the positions. Without it the server returns 412 + a preview and changes nothing.

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Optional free-text reason recorded in the audit event.

AutoTradeCloseOpenPositionsRequest.title: AutoTradeCloseOpenPositionsRequest
AutoTradeCloseOpenPositionsRequest.description:
Request body for the manual ``/auto-trade/close-positions`` flatten flow.

The flow is intentionally two-step:

  1. Client sends ``confirm: false`` (or omits it). The server returns
     HTTP 412 with a preview of what would be closed — list of positions,
     their sides/quantities, and how many conditional orders would be
     cancelled. Nothing changes on the exchange or in the DB.
  2. Client reviews the preview, then re-sends with ``confirm: true``.
     The server proceeds to cancel TP/SL conditional orders and market-
     close every open ``AutoTradePosition`` for the resolved scope.

Auto-trade ``is_running`` is **not** flipped automatically. Stop and close
are independent operations — the user explicitly asked for them to be
decoupled. Pending signals in the queue are also untouched.

AutoTradeClosedPositionInfo.title: AutoTradeClosedPositionInfo
AutoTradeClosedPositionInfo.description: Per-position outcome row in the success response.

kpi_guard_max_daily_loss_usdt.title: Kpi Guard Max Daily Loss Usdt

kpi_guard_max_daily_loss_pct.title: Kpi Guard Max Daily Loss Pct

kpi_guard_min_win_rate_pct.title: Kpi Guard Min Win Rate Pct

AutoTradeRiskConfig.title: AutoTradeRiskConfig
AutoTradeRiskConfig.description:
Pre-Trade Risk Engine limits for a strategy (W8).

Every limit is optional; ``None`` means "rule off". The model is used both
as nested input on the config upsert and (with ``from_attributes``) as the
serialized form of the :class:`app.models.auto_trade_risk_config.AutoTradeRiskConfig` row on read. Bounds here mirror the table-level
CheckConstraints so a bad value is rejected at the API edge with a clear
422 rather than surfacing as a database error.

OaCalibrationResponse.title: OaCalibrationResponse
OaCalibrationResponse.description: Combined OA view served to the per-profile UI panel (S7).

OaProfileAccuracy.title: OaProfileAccuracy
OaProfileAccuracy.description: A profile's forecast accuracy for one window (executed + shadow).

OaProfileCalibration.title: OaProfileCalibration
OaProfileCalibration.description: A profile's probability calibrator + its out-of-sample quality (S7).

OaReliabilityBin.title: OaReliabilityBin
OaReliabilityBin.description: One bin of the reliability (calibration) diagram.

PositionTraceRead.title: PositionTraceRead
PositionTraceRead.description:
Post-Trade execution trace for one position (W9 — T3.1).

The signal→close timeline: position metadata + linkage pointers (the AI
``decision_event_id`` points at core's ``ai_decision_events`` and is surfaced,
not dereferenced) + the chronological ``AutoTradeEvent`` list.

PromotionGateCriterionRead.title: PromotionGateCriterionRead
PromotionGateCriterionRead.description: One promotion-gate criterion with its actual value vs threshold (B5).

PromotionStatusRead.title: PromotionStatusRead
PromotionStatusRead.description: Current lifecycle stage + KPI-Gate readiness for a strategy (B5 — W10).

RiskConfigBulkApplyResponse.title: RiskConfigBulkApplyResponse
RiskConfigBulkApplyResponse.description: Result of applying one risk config to all of a user's strategies (A2).

max_dd_pct.title: Max Dd Pct
max_dd_pct.description: Max drawdown as a % of the per-trade notional base (position_size_usdt), NOT account equity — a W9 proxy pending real-balance calibration. UI: label accordingly; do not present as account drawdown.

roi_pct.title: Roi Pct
roi_pct.description: Realized PnL as a % of the per-trade notional base (position_size_usdt), NOT account equity — a W9 proxy (can read high). Calibrate the base to the sub-account balance before relying on it; UI must label the denominator.

StrategyHealthRead.title: StrategyHealthRead
StrategyHealthRead.description: On-read composite health for one strategy (W8 — T2.1).

max_dd_pct.title: Max Dd Pct
max_dd_pct.description: Max drawdown as a % of the per-trade notional base, NOT account equity (W9 proxy). UI must label the denominator; calibration pending.

roi_pct.title: Roi Pct
roi_pct.description: Realized PnL as a % of the per-trade notional base, NOT account equity (W9 proxy — can read high). UI must label the denominator; calibration pending.

kpi_as_of.title: Kpi As Of
kpi_as_of.description: When the surfaced KPIs were computed: the snapshot's computed_at when read from the cron-written snapshot, or the live recompute time when the snapshot was missing/stale for a running strategy. Null when no KPIs.

StrategyPortfolioEntryRead.title: StrategyPortfolioEntryRead
StrategyPortfolioEntryRead.description:
One strategy slot in the aggregated portfolio view.

Each entry corresponds to a single :class:`AutoTradeConfig` row, which by
W7 design owns its own exchange sub-account (credential). Balance values
are pulled live from the exchange; ``balance_error`` is populated when
that sub-account's adapter call failed so the dashboard can show a
degraded state instead of failing the whole portfolio fetch.

start_time.title: Start Time
start_time.description: Optional inclusive OHLCV start time (ISO datetime).

end_time.title: End Time
end_time.description: Optional inclusive OHLCV end time (ISO datetime).

candles.title: Candles
candles.description: Optional OHLCV override from client. If omitted, backend fetches candles by symbol/timeframe/bars.

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Selected indicators for VWAP builder. If empty, backend uses indicators from preset.
